subject: 50 years ago today, Apollo 11 launched for the Moon
body:
On July 20, 1969, humans walked on another world for the first time in history. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=60845af512&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 16, 2019
---
| | |
# **50 years ago today, Apollo 11 launched for the Moon**

**On July 20, 1969** , humans walked on another world for the first time in history. Four days earlier, on July 16, the Apollo 11 mission launched with Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins onboard.

**The Moon landing** achieved a goal that President John F. Kennedy set in 1961, before Americans had even orbited the Earth. After a landing that included dodging a lunar crater and a boulder field just before touchdown, Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin explored the area around their landing site for more than two hours.

**They collected soil** and rock samples, planted an American flag, and left behind a plaque with a simple message for the heavens: “We came in peace for all mankind.”

**For this historic anniversary** , the National Air and Space Museum held a special ceremony today, displaying the same space suit that mission commander Armstrong wore when he took that “giant leap for mankind.”

** **** NASA: **[At 9:32 a.m. ET, Apollo 11 launched, sending 3 astronauts to the Moon](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cc550f0220&e=3ee4985eea)

**“At that moment,** the nation held its breath—a nation that had been deeply divided during the tumultuous 1960s,” Vice President Mike Pence said during the unveiling ceremony. “On top of the contributions to science and human understanding, for that brief moment, the man who wore this suit brought together our nation and the world.”

**Neil Armstrong** was known as ‘Ice Commander’ among his colleagues, a testament to his strength of character and ability to remain calm under pressure. His iron will was on full display while touching down on the lunar surface, during which he likely saved the lives of his two crewmembers. The designated landing area, it turns out, was full of boulders large enough to potentially doom the entire mission. Armstrong calmly took control of the module and found a safe spot to land with only 17 seconds of fuel left to spare.

**“The debt** **this nation** owes to our Apollo astronauts, including the man who wore the suit that we unveil today, we can never fully repay,” the Vice President said.

**Heroes** such as the Apollo 11 crew helped pave the way for more achievements by the men and women of NASA. Under President Donald J. Trump, the best is still to come.

**America** is renewing its commitment to be the sole leader in space activity and exploration. After remaining dormant for the past quarter-century, the National Space Council was given new life by President Trump. NASA now has a renewed mission: to return America to the moon within the next five years—and, from there, on to Mars.

**“I have a feeling** that the man who wore the suit that we will unveil today would be glad to know that the first woman and the next man on the moon will also be an American,” the Vice President said.

[Relive the Apollo 11 launch, 50 years later.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1ce353c929&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump says Congressional Dems “should love our country”**

**At a Cabinet meeting** this morning, President Trump told Americans exactly what he expects from members of the United States Congress.

**“They can go wherever** they want or they can stay. But they should love our country,” the President said. “They shouldn’t hate our country.”

** Watch: **[“It’s up to them,” says President Trump.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=63e5a7c903&e=3ee4985eea)

**“I have clips** right here,” he continued. “The most vile, horrible statements about our country, about Israel, about others . . . You have the same list that I do.”

**Here are a few** of those statements from a prominent group of far-left, freshmen Democratic Congresswomen:
* Among other anti-Semitic tropes, Rep. Ilhan Omar said that Jewish lawmakers in the United States “have allegiance to a foreign country.”

* Rep. Omar has lied that American soldiers killed “thousands of Somalis” during the 1993 Black Hawk Down mission. She has also blamed the United States for terrorist attacks and the socialist humanitarian crisis in Venezuela.

* Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez says that illegal immigrants are “more American than any person who seeks to keep them out ever will be.”

* Rep. Ocasio-Cortez claimed that U.S. law enforcement officers are running “concentration camps” on the southern border and used the phrase “never again”—a refrain strongly associated with remembering the Holocaust.

**To top it off** , when asked to condemn the [Antifa terrorist attack](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cc2f996489&e=3ee4985eea) at a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement facility in Washington state this past weekend, Rep. Ayanna Pressley [attacked the media](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=30669bd26a&e=3ee4985eea), Rep. Ocasio-Cortez was [silent](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8f84f3c8ac&e=3ee4985eea), and Rep. Omar was [silent](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0fcdc2c4d3&e=3ee4985eea).

**Democratic leaders** and the mainstream media don’t want to talk about any of this. President Trump isn’t going to let them ignore it and hope that no one notices.
---

|

name: Bobby Jindal
subject: New Op-ed: "Trump's Foes are Crazier Than He Is."
body:
I discuss that while craziness that exists on both sides of the aisle, that on the Left could have far more disastrous effects. [ ](http://mail.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_e=2oF2RHLkw9k5DWj2xJp-x0b4pfH0nIqH_sG-7PDZx8TpE5w76_st7LLomb5b882GVWsYkKXelpC3ruy_uq3r6MbqMWKNVXbyn5jrwuKmvNlrG_ayBTowS3KeXhBwzJIR_i52VtjIzojjEQgDL2N46kvW1ECEQoAZ0tUHnPT3f17BGBDZhj8rtXcBSOJYJIW5d8cgiZ8u2DFjx67BJpAM29Cg8SfWxkxvGl2koYgJneQBoXE2u0OsYv7vLJq0Be7HwTBfDWDDkz4UWXXFxUo5GA%3D%3D) Hello Everyone, In case you missed it, I want to make sure you have a chance to read my Op-Ed in the _Wall Street Journal_ titled “[Trump’s Foes are Crazier Than He Is](http://mail.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_e=2oF2RHLkw9k5DWj2xJp-x-jMZoWQxswUMSTwVOuIYGqrcLg2EJ9H6VgENHCDevKppRvMYNLAepieSs5ag0jFJfwYSaAJl2PYNVehvg8aTJvD2O1UxVtxB_YBLNito9Pyx07lZgi87FdJqNSfE-oU0lmkFKThTn6J1CXNndRrxZICMsNbaqyJUgoMmTpI-edQT56JbSF1usxKKbTAQdlEiNkNywaDGhU_iltBo1nLpJWi3sxD7mUpkwsKEqBn5fW2Edtu04tUbP3I1JOeUhx5ElWYlVdKKGlALXsGy4G0Nz7qxykl4Fc_exlyDlXL23gS1HD2UdhdT4owL_U3-Syn8w%3D%3D).” In it, I discuss that while craziness that exists on both sides of the aisle, that on the Left could have far more disastrous effects. Politics seem more chaotic, polarized and extreme than ever. There is seemingly no time to rest between the Kavanaugh hearings, the border crisis, and multiple trade wars, just to name a few. _And those in charge can hardly claim they’re making things better._ [ ](http://mail.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_e=2oF2RHLkw9k5DWj2xJp-x-jMZoWQxswUMSTwVOuIYGqrcLg2EJ9H6VgENHCDevKppRvMYNLAepieSs5ag0jFJfwYSaAJl2PYNVehvg8aTJvD2O1UxVtxB_YBLNito9Py1qQwrVuSRdsEfFxHCq1hUOqMuSrYeCYVFfmk_qGyu2LWBmCV5Qd6QNQ8bsZmB2SifHwUsyzZWtgSFSOv6bU56xhTIpKw71yJyrUFlEnHnroK4YCnxkm5tNyCZXkHcLW1N9CVpAsTBjOTij_SD9GYkaaz_qm5yPH_YNa90VH9uUeJMSroVImxRNwkmO0y_f4NoDxSpcm7neTpQD7q_9AMmw%3D%3D) [ Read the full article here ](http://mail.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_e=2oF2RHLkw9k5DWj2xJp-x-jMZoWQxswUMSTwVOuIYGqrcLg2EJ9H6VgENHCDevKppRvMYNLAepieSs5ag0jFJfwYSaAJl2PYNVehvg8aTJvD2O1UxVtxB_YBLNito9PyRG0qc-4vhmmhhXhtGoLMWxylC6d6C7QNygH5oJp5BzRoQZYzDqwTxL66HiKgAvwzMgbHgfQPRdra2W2Q47T41I6cRXMIMT10VRF2lW1JCXivoA_L5JoCn7M7sZr4H8KTDiT8ImUUcEqr-n8miY4px91tvVekZwS_FTgxF8n8fULZN_afDYGEy1D_yCgUVqe3361MrRecdQ0SCC1FHBL-RA%3D%3D) The craziness on the Republican side is easy to identify: our President’s penchant for personal attacks and undisciplined tweets drives even some of his supporters crazy. All sides can agree that it lies in the President’s personality and ego and not in his policies, which are working well in many cases.

From an inability to condemn anti-Semitism to embracing the radically expensive Green New Deal, the policies of the far-Left have the ability to change the fabric of our nation as we know it (and certainly not for the better). The damage alone to our economy from these costly policies could be irreversible.

Most Americans aren’t eager to embrace either party’s craziness, but sensible swing voters should prefer the Republican version. Rhetoric and Tweets can be shocking, but the disruptive force of nature currently occupying the White House will eventually pass from the scene, and his unique mannerisms with him. The damage being done to the Democratic Party is much more substantial and will last far longer.

[Click here to read more](http://mail.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_e=2oF2RHLkw9k5DWj2xJp-x-jMZoWQxswUMSTwVOuIYGqrcLg2EJ9H6VgENHCDevKppRvMYNLAepieSs5ag0jFJfwYSaAJl2PYNVehvg8aTJvD2O1UxVtxB_YBLNito9Py3oYBtz9V9BbCVu6lYvxDeEmC3vVngqkvUwUtsB6iGtazxywnOj0uwMX-bUHTg21ZohvfVQ_wxMLoNxSH-5v6RP5X7HrzPI3mJdDEoOV3SCXBKX4Lna6YnWTJPHQdexc3JWR5HqZsYpgy_piaRf3JtT1b4IsQ6vbqn-guOs6uoRxoL1P1qAT4SZAQ2cm6sXu6fsSrZ8U4CPegJAA5PBVWUw%3D%3D), and let me know what you think. [ Read the full article here ](http://mail.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_e=2oF2RHLkw9k5DWj2xJp-x-jMZoWQxswUMSTwVOuIYGqrcLg2EJ9H6VgENHCDevKppRvMYNLAepieSs5ag0jFJfwYSaAJl2PYNVehvg8aTJvD2O1UxVtxB_YBLNito9PyVbr9ryLFi1_3EMGBci-UEDIOLaRlq3hIVpDPoTbInkgKU1XiJH1jKx3DMCx_Eugv9Dlbpc3KSMEUl0T01VutRH52RRBMZXM-owwUnPg31o5Cs1rnuehQ1P-51_uxamY0nIknkc5nJ9mEletbgJ-ZFEHroniEMMqZpTStXDK7b0e593RgupTpyZPJVX4aImtfnwjqVao3OvMLYUfC03jb9g%3D%3D) Sincerely,

Bobby Jindal [ UNSUBSCRIBE HERE ](http://mail.americanxt.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_s=d00704aef8444d6ab1c3b56baf4ffac0&_t=eCddHko8My0PxhUWY9W_HFdLtTZ9aARc_x5TmMpBoKS1-DloLL7XmpqlzgVdhFuv1GimtrkiHSTmCDLCkAJi20f3l7eVNdR3Y0BrciyAd0vdotfo0kMYR5D1HswgWzxE-jTlQ33BvPkYIflnDEpMSAakZrLvFriEQSUtfg2JUDs%3D) Email sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by America Next PO Box 320966, Alexandria, VA 22320

name: Abigail
subject: women’s soccer and equal pay
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/JUmwsyjHaDEWvKldLXHaqbnA4UrbUY09VOSEYWbLntw=/sx_j9yHtE_4Md0pasMUIpjBRBtOQgnf_TE_1RB7bjk4=?refcode=BLU-footer)
---
Peter,

Like so many of you, I was incredibly proud of the U.S. Women's National Team as they competed in the World Cup and took home _another_ victory for the United States.

[**_And as our team won the World Cup, chants of "EQUAL PAY" broke out in the stadium._**](https://act.myngp.com/el/JUmwsyjHaDEWvKldLXHaqbnA4UrbUY09VOSEYWbLntw=/0QueJzZEiAvwt_ta1cxssSDD3ETHIL46KOAhJXxdml4=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190717-1-EM&amount=25&amounts=25,50,100,250,500)

Peter, equal pay might be in the national spotlight at the moment, but it's an issue that women in Virginia face _every single day_.

In the Commonwealth, the average woman working full time makes 78 cents for each dollar her male counterpart makes. For women of color, it's even less. African American women are paid 60 cents and Latinas are paid 53 cents. **And by one estimate, Virginia women who work full time lose a combined total of $13 billion each year because of the pay gap.**

This is one of the reasons I co-sponsored the Paycheck Fairness Act. It's about ensuring that families - in particular, the women who are supporting families - have the ability to earn pay equal to their male counterparts.

I'm going to continue to work for equal pay for women and the families that depend on it. Not just because it's in the spotlight now, but because of all the families across Virginia that deserve it.

[**_As I continue to work for all Virginians, I hope that you 'll consider a donation to my campaign >>>_**](https://act.myngp.com/el/JUmwsyjHaDEWvKldLXHaqbnA4UrbUY09VOSEYWbLntw=/0QueJzZEiAvwt_ta1cxssSDD3ETHIL46KOAhJXxdml4=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190717-1-EM&amount=25&amounts=25,50,100,250,500)

I was thrust into the spotlight when Donald Trump insulted me, my loving wife, and our fallen son.

I was reminded of that painful moment this week when Trump told four Democratic women of color to "go back" to their "crime infested" countries.

Every news outlet -- even Fox News -- says Democrats can defeat Mitch McConnell and win the Senate. But we must win in New Mexico to make it happen.

And I can't think of a better way to take out McConnell's Senate than by electing people of color like Ben Ray.

subject: President Trump's Asylum Action Will Help Ease the Border Crisis
body:
“This week, the Trump administration continued its efforts to bring order to the southern border by imposing new rules on the asylum-seeking process. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=00519aeff9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump's Asylum Action Will Help Ease the Border Crisis**

“This week, the Trump administration continued its efforts to bring order to the southern border by imposing new rules on the asylum-seeking process. This is what was needed,” the _Washington Examiner_ editorial board writes.

Under the new rule, an alien who enters or attempts to enter the United States across the southern border, but who passed through other countries on the way without applying for protections from persecution or torture, will be ineligible for asylum in America.

Our asylum laws were “not supposed to be an invitation to unlimited economic migration. But that is how the law is currently functioning, with Central Americans making their way here by the tens of thousands every month. They have learned exactly how to push the system's buttons in order to secure long-term permission to be inside the U.S., and this is why detention centers near the border are overflowing.”

Meanwhile, Democrats in Congress have “shown no sense of urgency to fix the problem.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=442b24bb6b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “If the USMCA were brought to the floor of the House for a vote, it would pass overwhelmingly. Yet, House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) continues to slow-walk the ratification process. She can’t take yes for an answer because too many in her party only want to say no,” Rep. Ron Estes (R-KS) writes in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bac00aeccf&e=3ee4985eea). “The sad reality is too many in Pelosi’s party would gladly hand America a loss if it means denying President Trump a win.”
---
|
---
| “Sen. Josh Hawley (R-MO) scolded a Google executive Tuesday, saying that Congress’ patience with Google’s monopoly is running thin, and that it’s ‘time for accountability,’” Sean Moran reports for [_Breitbart_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4316b4fb28&e=3ee4985eea). “Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-CT) asked Google vice president of government affairs and public policy Karan Bhatia if he would submit to an independent, third-party audit of its content moderation practices, to which Bhatia said no.”
---
|
---
| “NAFTA, which was approved and has been in place since 1994, was written before many of the digital technologies that drive our 21st century global economy, such as cloud computing and online commerce, even existed,” business leader James Brett writes in the [_New Hampshire Business Review_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=53874a43c8&e=3ee4985eea). “Fortunately, our leaders in Congress have the opportunity to take an important step to bolster U.S. exports and drive continued economic growth by approving the new US-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA).”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1075c7a2b5&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=26345950ab&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=f96b9d761b)
---

name: Kamala HQ
subject: Some good news for you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
Kamala slayed in last month’s presidential debate -- it was incredible watching her speak truth to power about the values and the solutions we’re working so hard to advance in this election. Speaker Pelosi **correctly noted** that we should all stand together to rebuke racism in government:
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0719/72412298/439f28e0/610209b8/2223857016/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/thank-you-house-democrats?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190717_ThankPelosi_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190717_ThankPelosi_U1_X1")

name: Michael Bennet
subject: universal health care is possible
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Team, when I decided to run for president, I knew achieving universal healthcare coverage would be one of my top priorities.

I hope you'll give me a few moments to explain my plan for how to get there.

[It's called Medicare-X, and it's the fastest and best way to achieve universal health care for all.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad50/134673f2/faec5b7/69968595/1379072582/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

Medicare-X creates a public option -- an insurance plan available on the exchange that gives people the choice of keeping their current insurance or purchasing a more affordable public plan. Medicare-X, for the first time in history, requires the federal government to negotiate lower prescription drug prices. And it will force private insurers to compete and lower premiums across the board, especially in rural areas.

When it comes to expanding health coverage for millions of Americans, I don't think banning private insurance is the answer.

180 million Americans don't want to give up the insurance they receive through their employer.

20 million seniors don't want their Medicare Advantage stripped away.

Union members don't want their negotiated plans eliminated.

Medicare for All would do all of that.

My Medicare-X plan gives Americans the choice to decide what's right for their families.

[Team, I want to hear from you. Do you support my plan to achieve universal coverage with Medicare-X?](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad50/134673f2/faec5b7/69968595/1379072582/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

| | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad50/134673f2/faec5b7/69968595/1379072582/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---

Medicare for All may sound good at first, but when voters understand that it bans all private insurance--including employer and union plans--and dramatically raises their taxes, public support plummets.

And that could mean Democrats losing key states in the 2020 election.

We need a healthcare agenda with overwhelming support that voters can rally behind so we can beat Donald Trump in 2020; that plan is Medicare-X.

Team, Medicare-X is the fastest way to achieve universal health coverage - in a way that works best for all Americans.

[Please sign on and let me know if you're committed to making progress on health care now >>](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad50/134673f2/faec5b7/69968595/1379072582/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

| [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad50/134673f2/faec5b7/69968595/1379072582/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---

Thanks for joining the fight,

Michael

name: Michael Bennet
subject: universal health care is possible
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Team, when I decided to run for president, I knew achieving universal healthcare coverage would be one of my top priorities.

I hope you'll give me a few moments to explain my plan for how to get there.

[It's called Medicare-X, and it's the fastest and best way to achieve universal health care for all.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad51/134673f2/fb85dad/699685af/693016836/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_SDB&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

Medicare-X creates a public option -- an insurance plan available on the exchange that gives people the choice of keeping their current insurance or purchasing a more affordable public plan. Medicare-X, for the first time in history, requires the federal government to negotiate lower prescription drug prices. And it will force private insurers to compete and lower premiums across the board, especially in rural areas.

When it comes to expanding health coverage for millions of Americans, I don't think banning private insurance is the answer.

180 million Americans don't want to give up the insurance they receive through their employer.

20 million seniors don't want their Medicare Advantage stripped away.

Union members don't want their negotiated plans eliminated.

Medicare for All would do all of that.

My Medicare-X plan gives Americans the choice to decide what's right for their families.

[Team, I want to hear from you. Do you support my plan to achieve universal coverage with Medicare-X?](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad51/134673f2/fb85dad/699685af/693016836/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_SDB&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

| | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad51/134673f2/fb85dad/699685af/693016836/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_SDB&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---

Medicare for All may sound good at first, but when voters understand that it bans all private insurance--including employer and union plans--and dramatically raises their taxes, public support plummets.

And that could mean Democrats losing key states in the 2020 election.

We need a healthcare agenda with overwhelming support that voters can rally behind so we can beat Donald Trump in 2020; that plan is Medicare-X.

Team, Medicare-X is the fastest way to achieve universal health coverage - in a way that works best for all Americans.

[Please sign on and let me know if you're committed to making progress on health care now >>](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad51/134673f2/fb85dad/699685af/693016836/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_SDB&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

| [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbad51/134673f2/fb85dad/699685af/693016836/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/MedicareX?source=EM_PET_B1_071719_SDB&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

Michael

name: Team Wilmot
subject: Support Wilmot Collins and fight Steve Daines' dangerous rhetoric
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Team, now is the time to take a stand and show that we are better than divisiveness and name-calling from Senator Daines and President Trump. As Americans, we know that behavior like this is hateful and unacceptable.

[**Want to fight back? Contribute $5 to help elect Wilmot Collins.**](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190717&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

**[contribute](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190717&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
\------Original message from Wilmot Collins------
Team,
Not long after I came to Helena as a refugee after fleeing the Liberian civil war, someone spray painted "go back to Africa" on our garage door.
Those words stung -- but our Montana neighbors rallied around us and washed our garage door. I know the true spirit of Montana because the vast majority of Montanans showered my family and I with kindness and generosity. This country gave us an amazing life and I spend every day trying to show my gratitude for this great nation.
But Republican Senator Steve Daines has lost touch with who we are as Americans. After President Trump tweeted hateful comments encouraging four congresswomen of color to "go back" where they came from, Senator Daines rushed to his defense.
[**If you believe that we are better than Senator Daines ' divisiveness and name-calling, contribute $5 now to join our movement to defeat him.**](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190717&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
While three of the congresswomen President Trump attacked were born in the U.S., Congresswoman Ilhan Omar was born in Somalia and came here as a refugee. Like her, I came to the U.S. seeking freedom and opportunity.
After arriving in Montana, I served this country for over 20 years in the U.S. Armed Forces and have sought to contribute to my community any way I can. Does Senator Daines think I should "go back to where I came from" too?
Whatever Senator Daines thinks, we aren't going anywhere. I will continue to serve this country until the day I die, along with so many other refugees and children of immigrants who love this country.
While Senator Daines is trying to distract Montanans from his atrocious record on issues like health care and public lands, we are focusing on fixing problems.
[**We deserve better leadership in Washington than we 're seeing from Senator Daines. Contribute $5 to defeat him and flip this Senate seat.**](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190717&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Thank you,
Wilmot
**[contribute](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190717&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

* * *

name: Team Beto
subject: Falling behind on fundraising
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
It’s critical for us to pick up the fundraising pace right now. If we don’t, we could lose ground to others who simply have more money to spend.
#
A handful of candidates outraised us in the second quarter — some by quite a lot. And since grassroots fundraising has always been a key strength for this campaign, it’s important for us not to slip too far behind.
It looks like we’ll be heavily outspent in the next few months. Other candidates will likely have more ads on TV and online. They’ll have the ability to put more staff on the ground in key states. And some of them have even started super PACs to spend in their favor.
**It’s critical for us to pick up the fundraising pace right now.** If we don’t, we could lose ground to others who simply have more money to spend.
[Can you rush a $3 donation to Beto right now to help us close the gap? Your contributions now will play a key part in deciding whether or not we are victorious in the first primaries and caucuses.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18156?refcode=ema20190717-43097&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=43097%2E444211%2ErwdoJ3)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18156?refcode=ema20190717-43097&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=43097%2E444211%2ErwdoJ3)**
Everyone will be watching closely to see what we can fundraise on the next big report. They want to see if this campaign can compete with some of the other candidates bringing in big checks and relying on super PACs. Whether or not we can largely comes down to how many people step up right now.
You see, emails like this one are responsible for about half of all the money we bring in to this campaign. So whether it’s $5 or $50, every single donation adds up to give us the critical resources we need.
[Let’s show them what we’re made of. Make a quick donation to Beto right now to help us close the gap.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18156?refcode=ema20190717-43097&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=43097%2E444211%2ErwdoJ3)
\- Team Beto

If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=22&akid=43097%2E444211%2ErwdoJ3). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

| Friend: One week from today, Robert Mueller will testify on Capitol Hill about his investigation into Donald Trump.

After Mueller's press conference in May, where he refused to clear Trump of potential crimes, I made my position clear:

**Congress has a responsibility to get the facts. We also need to focus on the serious and ongoing threat to our election security and national security from Russia 's interference in our elections, which threatens our democracy. We must urgently work to secure our elections, a challenge that Trump has failed to meet or even acknowledge.**

Mueller's testimony underscores the urgency of the moment. We have to beat Trump at the ballot box.

**If you agree that the American people deserve a President who will ensure the security of our democracy, chip in $5 right now to help us expand our campaign.** ****[](https://secure.hickenlooper. subject: they spent HOW much??
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beshear/Coleman 2019
body:
$1.5 million. That's how much Matt Bevin and his big money pals have spent on attack ads against us since Andy won his primary on May 21.
|
---

| | Peter, I'd like to drop a number on you: $1.5 million. That's how much Matt Bevin and his big money pals have spent on attack ads against us since Andy won his primary on May 21. | [](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557b34/ad6dc73/155afb48/db402b2/4179883400/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE2MSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-attackfund?refcode=20190717nd__161__&amount=5")
---
**[Can you donate right now and help us build up our attack ad fund? We've got to push back against Matt Bevin's dark money-funded attacks before it's too late.](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557b34/ad6dc73/155afb48/db402b2/4179883400/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE2MSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-attackfund?refcode=20190717nd__161__&amount=5")**

$1.5 million is a **LOT** of money, which makes us wonder: What is Matt Bevin so scared of?

subject: American workers have waited long enough
body:
“NAFTA’s shortcomings were evident when signed,” then-Sen. Barack Obama said on the campaign trail in 2008. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=14bb397bb4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 17, 2019
---
| | |
# **American workers have waited long enough**

**“NAFTA’s shortcomings** were evident when signed,” then-Sen. Barack Obama said on the campaign trail in 2008. **“** We must now amend the agreement to fix them.”

**He didn’t**. Instead, Washington continued its long tradition of paying lip service to American workers—and then turning around and standing up for global capital instead.

**President Donald J. Trump** kept his promise. Now it’s time for Congress to keep theirs.

**Last November** , President Trump joined our North American allies in signing the new U.S.–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA), a trade deal that modernizes NAFTA while securing stronger protections for American labor. Last month, Mexico’s legislature became the first to overwhelmingly pass the new deal into law. Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau traveled to Washington the very next day, urging Democrat leaders in Congress to join President Trump in making USMCA a reality.

**American workers** have waited long enough for Washington to fix NAFTA. “That outdated trade deal has hurt middle-class Americans, stifled innovation and left entire communities shuttered as jobs and opportunity have moved south of the border,” Vice President Mike Pence wrote in _The Washington Post_ over the weekend.

**USMCA guarantees** the strongest labor protections of any U.S. trade agreement. It ensures fair wages in all three countries and gives Mexican workers real collective bargaining rights—a move that finally puts American unions on a level playing field.

**The new deal** also eliminates the NAFTA loophole that allows foreign car companies to buy auto parts from China or Europe, assemble them in Mexico, and then sell those vehicles duty-free in the United States. With USMCA, “we will require at least 75 percent of a car to be built with parts genuinely made in North America for it to be sold duty-free,” the Vice President writes.

**“And for the first time ever** , we will require at least 40 percent of a duty-free car to be made by workers earning at least an average of $16 an hour.”

**In addition to factory workers** , USMCA will boost America’s farmers, ranchers, energy producers, digital and technology workers, and other middle-class families.

**“For years, members of Congress** have demanded a replacement for NAFTA. Now they finally have the best replacement that they could ever even imagine,” President Trump told workers in Wisconsin last Friday.

**“So on behalf** of our nation’s loyal, hardworking citizens, I’m calling on Congress to pass USMCA and send it to my desk immediately for signature. We shouldn’t be playing around.”

[Vice President Pence: Congress must pass the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5224f8ceff&e=3ee4985eea)

**Rep. Ron Estes (R-KS):** [On the USMCA, Pelosi can't take yes for an answer](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9e9f107318&e=3ee4985eea)

The House, for the first time in a decade, passed legislation designed to address climate change and formed a Select Committee on the Climate Crisis.
**Multiple Presidential candidates** are calling climate change a "crisis" and an "emergency" with more than a dozen supporting a Democratic debate on climate change as well championing a Green New Deal.
This is our chance to send a message to Donald Trump, every Republican who denies the science and reality of climate change, and the entire world: **We WILL step up to act because this is an existential threat and a national emergency.**

### [S](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-j/)[omething Different](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-t/)
I'm going to do something a little different this week. As you know, I usually find some topic, or event, or issue, that I think is timely, and interesting, and write about it. This week I'm asking you to read something published over the weekend in the Cincinnati Enquirer.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-i/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-d/)
### [Are We Losing Our Country?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-h/)
President Trump once declared that a country without borders is not really a country anymore. And he was right. And that's just what seems to be rapidly happening to the greatest country this world has ever known.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-k/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-u/)
### [Did the Democrats Just Re-Elect President Trump at Their Debates Last Week?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-o/)
It is my humble opinion, that the Democrats, in their very first debate last week, went a considerable way towards re-electing President Trump in the upcoming 2020 election. I'm on the blog talking about it today.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [Here Come the Democrat Debates - Aaaaah!](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiktuho-kujkulhluu-p/)
Tonight and tomorrow night 20 Democrats are going to be debating each other. Each thinks he or she ought to be the next president of the United States. I'm on the blog talking about what we can expect.

Click the link below to read more.
